name: Shai-Hulud Self-Replicating npm Worm
description: Self-replicating worm that steals cloud tokens, deploys secret-scanning tools, and spreads to additional npm accounts. 640+ packages infected in first wave, 1092+ versions in Shai-Hulud 2.0. Affected packages from Zapier, AsyncAPI, PostHog, Postman, Browserbase.

affected_packages.name: @asyncapi/specs
affected_packages.note: First trojanized package, ~1.4M weekly downloads. Check npm advisory for specific affected versions.

c2_servers.note: Exfiltrates credentials to public GitHub repos with description 'Shai-Hulud: The Second Coming'

remediation: Check all npm packages for bun_environment.js or setup_bun.js files in node_modules
Verify SHA256 hashes of suspicious files against known IOCs
Review GitHub account for unrecognized public repositories named 'Shai-Hulud'
Rotate all cloud service tokens (AWS, GCP, Azure)
Rotate npm tokens and GitHub PATs
Run: npm audit for latest advisory data on affected packages
Enable 2FA on npm account if not already enabled
